clean_story: Hidden from view.  The space beyond sight. "Behind" marks what’s out of direct line—like the backside of a door, the far side of the moon, or the unseen gears in a clock. It’s where support happens:A stagehand moves props behind curtains. A tree’s roots spread underground. Your spine holds you upright—unseen but essential. Consider shadows. They stretch behind objects when light hits them, proving even emptiness has shape. Or echoes—sounds bouncing behind walls before returning to your ears. The word itself comes from Old English hindan, meaning "from the rear. "It’s not just location—it’s advantage. A predator stalks behind prey. A goalkeeper stands behind defenders. Even time has a "behind": history is what’s left behind us. Three related subjects are hidden systems, perspective shifts, and support roles.
